based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/mazur.golden.jsonl. Differs in exactly 4 places: the 3 fixture_status fields and the single targeted loss.total numeric field.

what_was_NOT_mutated: loss.per_output.o1 (0.274811083 — still consistent with 0.5 * (target.o1 - forward.o1.out)^2 individually; the contradiction is only at the SUM level)
targets.o1, targets.o2 (still the canonical Mazur values 0.01, 0.99)
forward.o1.out, forward.o2.out (still the engine's pinned values 0.751365070, 0.772928465)
all backward, all updates, parameters_after, post_update_forward, post_update_loss

single_target_invariant: Receipt declares loss.total = 0.298372109 but the per-output entries sum to 0.298371109 (= 0.5*(0.01-0.751365070)^2 + 0.5*(0.99-0.772928465)^2, the canonical Mazur half-squared-error). Rule 12 (half_squared_error branch) catches this: 'loss.total == sum(loss.per_output[*])' fails by 1e-6, well above the hybrid tolerance for that magnitude (~3e-10 = 1e-9 atol vs rtol-zero scalar form).
reconciliation_check_targeted_first: Rule 12 (loss formula consistency): loss.total must equal sum(loss.per_output[*]) under topology.loss='half_squared_error'.
purpose: Anti-circularity (Csmith/CompCert lineage) for the v0.4.2 Rule 12 (loss formula consistency) reconciler. Loss-side mutations are independent of backward-side computations: Rules 1-8 never read loss.total or loss.per_output, so prior to v0.4.2 a receipt could mutate loss.total arbitrarily and reconcileReceipt would return ok===true. Rule 12 closes that gap. This fixture is the minimum-viable bad case: a single +1e-6 mutation on loss.total that fires Rule 12 alone with no cascade to any other rule.
v0_4_2_note: v0.4.2 is the focused trust patch landing Rule 12 ahead of v0.5's softmax+CE wave. Rule 12 is the shared machinery: the half_squared_error branch ships in v0.4.2; cross_entropy_softmax branch lands with v0.5's softmax+CE engine path.
